summary: Tested vinyl player era switching (1970s/2000s/2020s/1940s). Slider tick changes pageIndex correctly and vinyl artwork swaps per era (verified via screenshot). Tonearm drag triggers vinyl spinning, lifting it stops the animation. However, I could NOT programmatically verify that the YouTube iframe actually loads the expected video IDs per era — postMessage-based loadVideoById does not change iframe.src, and infoDelivery messages from the YT iframe were not captured in headless Playwright. Code path is correct per source review.

integration_issues.issue: Could not capture YT iframe video_id via postMessage listener or YT.Player.prototype.loadVideoById hook in this Playwright/preview environment. Two YouTube iframes exist (one is the YT.Player audio host, src is the empty embed since loadVideoById uses postMessage). No '[YT] init'/'[YT] ready' console logs captured. Suggests either: (a) headless audio autoplay blocked so onReady never fires -> ytReadyRef stays false -> loadVideoById never called, OR (b) test instrumentation can't reach cross-origin YT state. Real-browser behavior is likely fine since prev agent verified all 12 IDs are embeddable.

action_items: Main agent should self-test in a real browser (not headless): switch slider to each era and drag tonearm; confirm audio actually plays for 1970s/2000s/2020s tracks. The code path (TRACKS_BY_ERA[1/2/3] -> playRandomTrackForEra -> ytPlayer.loadVideoById) is correct; behavior is gated on YT onReady firing which may be blocked in headless.

Optional: surface a visible 'now playing' title (e.g. read from getVideoData().title) so QA can verify which track loaded without needing audio. This would also future-proof against silently-failing IDs.
critical_code_review_comments: TRACKS_BY_ERA entries for eras 1, 2, 3 in /app/frontend/src/App.js (lines 83-103) match the IDs in the review request exactly: Queen 5zLnaNY58j8, ABBA 3qiMJt-JBb4, Bee Gees WlgoUlOkyzU, Donna Summer cZQ0q1s2y-s; Britney dm0ndgjk9V4, Eminem tR1ECf4sEpw, OutKast RqIRp4QE-1k, Coldplay q7mlB-adMBc; Weeknd XwxLwG2_Sxk, Dua Lipa j2c3tR_qfiQ, Olivia Rodrigo _Bjf-iExroI, Harry Styles Qfm6nfz1QNQ. Era 0 (1940s) untouched - no regression in code.

playRandomTrackForEra (line 215) correctly indexes TRACKS_BY_ERA[era] where era == pageIndex from useEffect at line 271-278. Random selection avoids replaying the same track and 30s timer (trackTimerRef) auto-rotates to next track. Logic is sound.
stopMusic at line 256 properly clears trackTimerRef, currentEraRef, currentTrackIdRef and calls stopVideo() when isSpinning becomes false (i.e., tonearm lifted below 30deg threshold).

Potential improvement: when YT onReady never fires (e.g., in restrictive embed contexts), playRandomTrackForEra recursively setTimeout-retries every 400ms forever while the disk spins, with no upper cap — minor resource issue.

The 'lift tonearm to stop' is gated on armAngle >= 30 -> isSpinning=true (line 359), but there is no symmetric path to set isSpinning=false from arm movement alone — it only stops when the user grabs the arm again (which sets isSpinning=false at line 444 via onArmMouseDown). This works because grabbing it again is the only way to physically move it back, but could feel non-intuitive.

success_rate.frontend: Era switching + spin/stop verified (4/4). YT audio verification: inconclusive (0/4 conclusively verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Headless Playwright cannot directly read the YT.Player state because (a) loadVideoById is postMessage-based so iframe.src stays at the empty embed URL, (b) cross-origin infoDelivery messages from the YT iframe weren't received in this preview env, and (c) hooking YT.Player.prototype.loadVideoById didn't take effect (likely because methods are added per-instance after the embed loads). Strategies that may work in future iterations: (1) add a visible 'now playing' badge in the UI that displays currentTrackIdRef.current — then Playwright can simply read its text; (2) attach a global window.__currentTrack ref inside playRandomTrackForEra for testability; (3) test in a non-headless browser. The era switching and spin/stop UI mechanics are confirmed working.
rca_of_the_issue: Not a bug — testing instrumentation limitation in headless cross-origin iframe scenario. The YT IFrame API is fundamentally cross-origin: parent can SEND postMessage commands (loadVideoById, etc) but only the YT.Player JS wrapper inside the parent window knows the current videoId — and accessing it requires holding a reference to the player instance, which lives in React's ytPlayerRef (not on window). Without exposing it on window or reflecting it in the DOM, no external test harness can verify which track is loaded.
